---
title: "Tempered Prior Paper"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(artfima)
```

# Regular ARIMA 
```{r}
y <- arima.sim(model = list(ar = 0.7, ma = 0.5), n = 200)
plot(y, main = "Simulated ARMA(1,1)", ylab = "y_t", type = "l")
```



```{r}
dat = artsim(n=100, d=0.2, lambda=3, phi=c(0.3), theta=c(0.2), mean=0, sigma2 = 1)
plot(x=1:100, y=dat, type='l')
```

```{r}
# Load necessary packages
library(artfima)
library(stats)   # for arima.sim and acf


# ------------------------
# 0. Parameters
# ------------------------

set.seed(123)
n <- 3000  # length of the time series
d_arfima <- .4  # fractional differencing parameter
lambda <- c(0, 0.001, 0.05, 0.1)  # smaller lambda faster decay
d_artfima <- 0.3
lag_max <- 30
# ------------------------
# 1. Simulate ARFIMA
# ------------------------
arfima_series <- artsim(n=n, d=d_arfima)
acf_arfima <- acf(arfima_series, plot = FALSE)

# ------------------------
# 2. Simulate ARTFIMA
# ------------------------

artfima_series <- artsim(n=n, d=d_arfima, lambda=d_artfima)
# artfima_series <- artfima.sim(n = n, model = list(d = d_artfima, lambda = lambda))
acf_artfima <- acf(artfima_series, plot = FALSE)

# ------------------------
# 3. Plot ACFs
# ------------------------
# Convert to numeric vectors for plotting
acf_ar <- acf_arfima$acf[1:(lag_max + 1)]
acf_at <- acf_artfima$acf[1:(lag_max + 1)]
lags <- 0:lag_max
acf0 <- acf_at[1]  # ACF at lag 0

plot(lags, acf_ar, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue", 
     ylim = c(0, max(acf_ar, acf_at)),
     xlab = "Lag", ylab = "ACF", main = "ARFIMA vs ARTFIMA ACF")
lines(lags, acf_at, type = "b", pch = 17, col = "red")
# Add to existing plot
legend("topright", 
       legend = c("ARFIMA", "ARTFIMA"),
       col = c("blue", "red"),
       pch = c(19,17), lty = c(NA, NA), lwd = c(NA, NA))

# Assuming previous plot is already made

```


```{r}
# =========================================
#  Amazon closing prices (2000-01-03 to 2017-01-03)
#  Data prep for ARTFIMA modeling
# =========================================

# Install required packages if not already installed
# if (!require("quantmod")) install.packages("quantmod", dependencies = TRUE)
# if (!require("tseries")) install.packages("tseries", dependencies = TRUE)

library(quantmod)
library(tseries)

# -----------------------------------------
# 1. Download Amazon stock data (symbol: AMZN)
# -----------------------------------------
getSymbols("AMZN", from = "2000-01-03", to = "2017-01-03")

# Extract adjusted closing prices
amzn_close <- Cl(AMZN)

# Plot closing prices
plot(amzn_close, main = "Amazon Closing Prices (2000–2017)", col = "darkblue")

# -----------------------------------------
# 2. Compute log returns
# -----------------------------------------
amzn_ret <- diff(log(amzn_close))
amzn_ret <- na.omit(amzn_ret)
amzn_sq <- amzn_ret^2

# Plot returns
plot(amzn_sq, main = "Amazon Square Log Returns (2000–2017)", col = "darkred")

# Basic summary
summary(amzn_sq)

# -----------------------------------------
# 3. Optional: check stationarity and lags
# -----------------------------------------
# adf.test(amzn_ret)
# par(mfrow = c(1,2))
acf(amzn_sq, lag.max = 100, main = "ACF of Amazon squared Log Returns")
# pacf(amzn_ret^2, lag.max = 100, main = "PACF of Amazon Log Returns")

# -----------------------------------------
# 4. Spectral Plot
# -----------------------------------------
spec <- spectrum(amzn_sq, spans = c(5,5), log = "no", main = "Spectral Density of Squared Log Returns (Amazon 2000–2017)")
plot(log(spec$freq), log(spec$spec),
     type = "p", col = "darkgreen",
     main = "Log–Log Spectral Density (Squared Log Returns)",
     xlab = "log(Frequency)", ylab = "log(Spectral Density)")

```
